title: Delivery circuit breaker: per-domain failure tracking, 1-hour pause
description:
Track consecutive delivery failures per target domain in-memory (HashMap<String, CircuitBreakerState>). After 10 consecutive failures to a domain: mark that domain's circuit as 'open' for 1 hour — skip deliveries to that domain until the hour expires. After 1 hour: try one delivery as 'probe'; on success, close circuit; on failure, extend by another hour.

Test discipline:
- 10 consecutive failures to example.com → circuit opens, subsequent deliveries skipped for that domain
- After 1 hour, probe attempt sent
- Probe success → circuit closes, deliveries resume
- Different domain unaffected by example.com's circuit

Defensive programming:
- Circuit state is in-memory only — lost on restart (acceptable, conservative behavior: open circuit = don't deliver)
- Don't let circuit state HashMap grow unbounded — prune entries for domains with no recent activity after 24h
acceptance_criteria: 10 failures opens circuit; other domains unaffected; circuit closes on successful probe after 1h
